summary: solidity-deploy-guard exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint at install time
details: package.json declares a lifecycle script (line 30) that invokes child_process and Node's https module to POST host data including hostname, cwd, and process.env contents to https://ddjidd564.github.io. A bundled scanner.js (5 files total) implements the full collector: it requires fs/os/https/child_process, gathers hostname/version/cwd/whoami output, and POSTs the resulting payload to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint (scanner.js lines 142-161, 234-249, 371). The destination is a GitHub Pages user site unrelated to any legitimate publisher, used here as an attacker-controlled drop. The package's stated purpose (a Solidity deploy 'guard') has no need to read host identity, working-directory paths, environment variables, or shell out to whoami, and certainly no need to ship them off-host. This is a credential and host-reconnaissance exfiltration payload firing on `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
